whatThisIs: Resolve which parent's health plan pays primary for a dependent child covered by both parents using the NAIC coordination-of-benefits birthday rule: the parent whose birthday (month and day, not year) falls earlier in the calendar year carries primary coverage; if both birthdays are the same, the plan that has covered the child longer is primary. Sophie Well takes the two birthdays plus optional coverage-effective dates and returns the primary plan with the reasoning.
whenToUse: Use this when a child has dual coverage and the family or biller needs to know which plan to bill first to avoid claim denials for missing primary-payer information. The birthday rule is the default for the majority of states and self-funded ERISA plans, but court-ordered coverage in custody decrees overrides it, and separated / divorced parents follow the custody-and-remarriage hierarchy rather than birthdays. Confirm against the actual SPDs when stakes are high.
